svg.title: The Guide to Wireframing — first 20 pages (FrameForge replica)
svg.desc: Structural replica of pp. 1–20 of UXPin's “The Guide to Wireframing” (Chris Bank). Body text is the real extracted copy; the book's photographic/sketch figures are the authors' copyrighted illustrations, shown as labelled image-placeholders. The cover's wireframe mock is rebuilt from primitives. Layout is representative, not pixel-exact.

text.tspan: High-Fidelity Media is another way you can make a wireframe more realistic.
This may mean filling in a picture, video, thumbnail, avatar, or background
image. This is becoming increasingly popular with the proliferation of
content-centric products, since the type of content can have a significant
impact on the overall structure and information hierarchy.
